prevalence.trend: increasing geographic spread with Aedes vectors; recurrent outbreaks in Northeast and Southeast

burden.mortalityRate: Low; disability burden can be substantial due to chronic arthralgia

riskFactors: standing water and poor waste management
delayed supportive care in severe cases
vulnerablePopulations: pregnant people and neonates (perinatal transmission risk)
communities in high Aedes density areas
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute fever with polyarthralgia; confirmation by RT-PCR (early) or IgM serology; differential includes dengue and Zika

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; syndromic surveillance with targeted lab confirmation
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Acute mortality low; long-term symptom control variable with chronic arthralgia in a subset

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS triage for arboviral syndromes; confirmatory testing prioritized during outbreaks
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care (analgesics, hydration); avoid NSAIDs until dengue excluded; management of chronic arthralgia with physiotherapy and rheumatology referral when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Chronic disability burden and limited multidisciplinary rehab access in SUS outside large centers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Integrated arbovirus surveillance and clinical guidance within SUS, with growing municipal vector control experience
